name: MPD Community Credit Union
description_short: MPD Community Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering checking, savings, loans, and online banking services to its community members since 1966.
description_long:
MPD Community Credit Union was founded in 1966 by eight community members (Johnnie Griggs Jr., Charles N. Kimbrough, David F. Bruce Jr., Charles E. Hay, Tillman A Fox, L. T. Dickens, Mildred M. Arnold, and Ernest W. Castleman) with a charter signed by the Tennessee Superintendent of Banks on November 7, 1966. The credit union began operations at 110 Public Square and has since grown to serve its community membership.

The institution offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products including checking accounts with rewards options, multiple savings account types, and online banking through their E-Credit Union platform. Their loan portfolio includes vehicle financing (as low as 5.35% APR), motorcycle loans (6.50% APR), recreational loans (7.00% APR), personal loans (7.50% APR), and Visa Classic credit cards (7.90% APR). Members can also access vehicle search tools, loan calculators, and educational resources through the website.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, MPD Community CU emphasizes member-centric service and community engagement. Their mission explicitly states a focus on engaging with members and community to support financial well-being. The organization provides free online banking services, 24-hour digital account access, and various member resources including newsletters and financial education.

The main limitation is that specific details about membership eligibility, fee structures, and account features are not fully detailed on the visible website content. Loan rates are advertised as "as low as" with asterisks indicating terms apply, but APR ranges and specific eligibility requirements are not comprehensively explained. The website appears to be under development in certain areas, with incomplete "About Us" content.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free online banking (E-Credit Union) with 24-hour account access including bill pay and fund transfers
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.35% APR for vehicle financing
Member-focused mission with explicit commitment to community engagement and financial well-being
Comprehensive loan options including vehicles, motorcycles, recreational, personal, and credit cards
Built-in vehicle search tool to help members find financing-ready vehicles
Mobile app availability with Card Suite Lite for credit card control and management
Multiple account types including checking with rewards and various savings options
cons: Fee schedule is referenced but not detailed on the website pages provided
Limited transparency on membership eligibility requirements and who can join
Loan rates shown as "as low as" with asterisks but specific terms, APR ranges, and eligibility criteria not fully disclosed
Website content appears incomplete (About Us section cuts off mid-sentence)
No information provided about account minimums, overdraft policies, or other key banking terms
best_for: Community members eligible for MPD CU membership seeking low-cost auto financing
Individuals wanting free online banking with bill pay and 24/7 account management
Members preferring a credit union structure over traditional banks for potential better rates and community focus
Vehicle buyers and owners looking for competitive motorcycle and recreational vehicle loans
diagnosis: MPD Community Credit Union is best for individuals who are eligible members of the credit union seeking competitive consumer lending and low-cost banking services. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is not clearly defined on their website, and specific terms, fees, and APR ranges for their products require direct contact with the institution.
services: Online banking (E-Credit Union) with bill pay
Visa Classic credit cards (7.90% APR minimum)
Mobile banking app with Card Suite Lite
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
